summary: Full E2E testing of new order management workflow (auth + 9-status state machine + revisions + reviews) and frontend buyer/seller integration. 21/21 backend pytest tests pass. Frontend buyer order creation, validation, tracking page, seller login/propose/chat, buyer accept, in-progress timer, cross-context updates all working. PIN restored to 503625.

issues: Order Aktif card runs '5' and '4 perlu tindakan' close together; text contrast and spacing acceptable but the subtitle 'X perlu tindakan' visually merges with the big number without enough gap. Minor polish only.

action_items: TESTID MISMATCH vs spec: spec referenced [data-testid=propose-days-input] and [data-testid=login-submit] but actual implementations are [data-testid=propose-days] and [data-testid=login-submit-button]. If the spec was sourced from PRD/external docs, either update the docs or rename the JSX testids for consistency. Not a functional bug.
Minor polish: add small horizontal gap or stacking between the big stat number and the '4 perlu tindakan' helper in [data-testid=stat-orders] on Dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 619-line file holding auth, orders, reviews, JWT, seed and state machine. Consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/orders.py, services/state_machine.py for maintainability once it grows further.
State machine transitions are guarded by ad-hoc status checks scattered across endpoints — a single allowed_transitions table or decorator would make future changes safer and easier to audit.

Revision quota check returns Indonesian error text ('Jatah revisi sudah habis untuk paket ini.') — fine for UI, but consider also returning a machine-readable error_code for the frontend to localize/branch on.

Order tracking token is generated and exposed in plaintext URL; for now acceptable (it's the design), but ensure it has enough entropy (looks ~32 chars random — OK) and there's no enumeration endpoint.
No rate limiting on /api/orders POST or /api/auth/login — easy DoS vector. Consider slowapi or a simple in-memory limiter for /auth/login at least.
Chat messages have no length cap visible in validation; long messages could bloat documents. Consider Pydantic max_length on chat content.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested critical flows (order create, validation, track, login, propose, chat, accept, in_progress, cross-context polling)

test_credentials: admin@website.id / PIN 503625 (verified restored at end of test run)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed orders and clean them up via DELETE /api/admin/orders/<code>. A small handful of buyer-form-created orders may remain (e.g. ORD-0HKOLZ from frontend test) and can be deleted via admin UI if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth endpoints, full order state machine (pending_review → awaiting_buyer → negotiating → in_progress → delivered → revision_requested loop → completed, plus rejected branch), revision quotas (basic=0, growth=1, pro=2), public /api/reviews + visibility toggle, admin list/delete — all verified via pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Frontend critical buyer + seller flow verified via Playwright. NOT explicitly retested in UI: full revision dialog flow on buyer track page, review submission UI on completed orders, negotiation dialog on buyer side, change-PIN UI (covered at API layer though). The actual testids in code are: propose-days (not propose-days-input), login-submit-button (not login-submit), login-email-input. Use these when scripting further UI tests.
rca of the issue: No bugs reproduced. The only 'issues' surfaced were two minor testid-name mismatches between the review spec and the actual JSX testids — these were worked around in the tests and reported as action items, not functional defects.
